• Any change or expansion of scope is a change of substance, including change to similar goods or services. Examples: Class 32 'beer' → Class 33 'spirits' (change); Class 12 'freight automobiles' → Class 12 'automobiles' (expansion).
• However, changing from a generic/collective description to specific individual goods or services is not a change of substance. Examples: Class 21 'tableware' → 'cups, teacups'; Class 41 'provision of recreational facilities' → 'provision of karaoke facilities, provision of other recreational facilities'.
• Narrowing scope, correcting errors, and clarifying unclear descriptions are not changes of substance.
• Retail services: changing between general retail services and specific retail services, or between retail services and goods, is a change of substance. Narrowing the range of goods handled by specific retail services is not.
(2) For the trademark (sign):
• Amendment of the trademark as recorded in the application is in principle a change of substance. Examples: changing, deleting, or adding words, devices, symbols, or three-dimensional shapes; changing colors.
• Exception: Deleting ancillary elements (e.g., JIS/JAS marks, place-of-origin indications) is not a change of substance.
• Adding or deleting the designation as a three-dimensional trademark, standard-character trademark, or new-type trademark is in principle a change of substance (with exceptions).

Article 16-2(3): Following a refusal of amendment decision, no registration decision may be issued within three months of service of the copy of the decision (within which period a new application may be filed).
Article 16-2(4): If a trial against the amendment refusal is requested, examination is suspended until the trial decision becomes final.
law.de:
Artikel 6 Abs. 1 (Ein Zeichen pro Anmeldung):
Jede Markenanmeldung muss für eine bestimmte Marke eingereicht werden. Unklare oder zu allgemeine Beschreibungen der Waren/Dienstleistungen führen zur Beanstandung.

Artikel 6 Abs. 2 (Klassenkonformität):
Designierte Waren/Dienstleistungen müssen dem Klassensystem (Klassen 1–45) entsprechen. Klassen 1–34 sind Waren-, Klassen 35–45 Dienstleistungsklassen.

Artikel 6 Abs. 3 (Klassen definieren keine Ähnlichkeit):
Das Klassensystem ist eine Verwaltungsklassifikation, keine Ähnlichkeitsdefinition. Ähnlichkeit wird anhand von Ähnlichkeitsgruppencodes bestimmt.

Artikel 16-2 (Wesensänderung und Zurückweisung):
Änderungen, die eine Wesensänderung darstellen, werden zurückgewiesen. Wesensänderung: Erweiterung/Änderung des Warenbereichs; Hinzufügung/Änderung/Löschung von Markenbestandteilen. Keine Wesensänderung: Einschränkung des Bereichs; Fehlerkorrektur; Änderung von Pauschalangaben zu spezifischen Waren.
law.it:
Articolo 6 comma 1 (Un marchio per domanda):
Ogni domanda di marchio deve essere presentata per un marchio specifico. Descrizioni vaghe o troppo generali dei prodotti/servizi portano a rilievi.

Articolo 6 comma 2 (Conformità alle classi):
I prodotti/servizi designati devono seguire il sistema di classi (Classi 1–45). Le classi 1–34 sono per prodotti, le classi 35–45 per servizi.

Articolo 6 comma 3 (Le classi non definiscono la somiglianza):
Il sistema di classi è una classificazione amministrativa, non una definizione di somiglianza. La somiglianza è determinata dai codici del gruppo di somiglianza.

Articolo 16-2 (Modifica sostanziale e rifiuto):
Le modifiche che costituiscono una modifica sostanziale vengono rifiutate. Modifica sostanziale: ampliamento/cambiamento dell'ambito dei prodotti; aggiunta/modifica/eliminazione di elementi del marchio. Non modifica sostanziale: riduzione dell'ambito; correzione di errori; cambiamento da descrizioni generiche a prodotti specifici.

practice.ja:
6条・16条の2の実務上のポイントは以下のとおり。

①指定商品・役務の記載精度が出願品質を左右する：「その他本類に属する商品」等のキャッチオール表示や、内容・範囲が不明確な記載は6条1項に基づく拒絶理由となる。出願前に類似商品・役務審査基準や商標法施行規則別表を参照して、明確かつ適切な表示を選定することが重要。

②区分（クラス）と類似は別概念：海外取引先に最も誤解されやすいポイント。「同じ区分だから類似」「別の区分だから非類似」という理解は誤り。類似判断は類似群コードによって行われるため、クリアランス調査では区分を超えた検索が必要。

③補正の限界を理解しておく：拒絶理由通知に対して手続補正書で対応する際、指定商品・役務の拡大・範囲変更や商標の実質変更は要旨変更として補正却下される。補正可能な範囲（減縮・誤記訂正・包括表示からの個別化等）を事前に理解しておくことが重要。

④包括表示から個別表示への変更は要旨変更でない：「食器類」→「コップ、茶わん」のような包括表示から個別商品への変更は要旨変更ではない。審査官から具体的な補正案が提示されることも多く、その場合は指示に従うことで拒絶理由を解消できる。

⑤補正却下後の救済手段：補正却下の決定謄本送達から3か月以内に、補正後の内容で新たに出願することが可能（17条の2準用意匠法17条の3）。ただし新出願の出願日は補正書提出時となり、原出願日には遡及しない点に注意。

⑥海外権利者への説明：日本の区分制度（45区分）はニース協定の国際分類に基づいており、EU・米国等と共通の枠組みだが、日本では類似判断が「類似群コード」により別途行われる点が異なる。区分の選択は類似群コードを考慮して行う必要があることを説明する。
practice.en:
Key practical points on Articles 6 and 16-2:

(1) The quality of designated goods/service descriptions determines application quality: Catch-all descriptions such as 'other goods in this class' or descriptions from which the scope cannot be clearly understood are grounds for refusal under Article 6(1). Before filing, refer to the Examination Standards for Similar Goods and Services and the Schedule to the Trademark Act Enforcement Regulations to select clear and appropriate descriptions.

(2) Class and similarity are distinct concepts: This is the most common misunderstanding among overseas clients. 'Same class therefore similar' and 'different classes therefore dissimilar' are both incorrect. Similarity assessment is based on similarity group codes, so clearance searches must extend across classes.

(3) Understand the limits of amendment: When responding to an office action with an amendment, expanding or changing the scope of designated goods or services or making substantive changes to the trademark will be treated as a change of substance and the amendment will be refused. The permissible range of amendment (narrowing, error correction, individualization of generic descriptions) should be understood in advance.

(4) Changing from a generic to a specific description is not a change of substance: Changing 'tableware' to 'cups, teacups' is not a change of substance. Examiners often suggest specific amendment text, and following the suggestion resolves the refusal.

(5) Relief after amendment refusal: A new application reflecting the amended content may be filed within three months of service of the amendment refusal decision (Article 17-2 incorporating Design Act Article 17-3 by reference). Note that the filing date of the new application is the date of the original amendment submission, not the original application date.

(6) Explaining to overseas clients: Japan's class system (45 classes) is based on the Nice Agreement international classification shared with the EU, US, and others, but Japan uses a separate 'similarity group code' system for similarity assessment. Class selection must therefore take similarity group codes into account.
practice.de:
Wichtige praktische Punkte zu Artikeln 6 und 16-2:

(1) Qualität der Beschreibungen entscheidet: Catch-all-Beschreibungen oder unklare Waren/Dienstleistungen führen zu Beanstandungen nach Artikel 6 Abs. 1.

(2) Klasse und Ähnlichkeit sind verschiedene Konzepte: Häufigster Irrtum bei ausländischen Mandanten. Ähnlichkeit wird anhand von Ähnlichkeitsgruppencodes bestimmt, nicht durch die Klasse.

(3) Änderungsgrenzen verstehen: Erweiterung des Warenbereichs oder wesentliche Markenänderungen sind Wesensänderungen und werden zurückgewiesen.

(4) Von Oberbegriff zu spezifischen Waren: Keine Wesensänderung. Prüfer schlagen oft spezifischen Korrekturtext vor.

(5) Abhilfe nach Zurückweisung der Änderung: Innerhalb von 3 Monaten neue Anmeldung möglich, aber mit Datum der ursprünglichen Änderungseinreichung.

(6) Erklärung gegenüber ausländischen Mandanten: Japan verwendet Ähnlichkeitsgruppencodes zur Ähnlichkeitsbeurteilung, nicht nur die Klasse.
practice.it:
Punti pratici chiave sugli articoli 6 e 16-2:

(1) La qualità delle descrizioni determina la qualità della domanda: Descrizioni generiche o poco chiare portano a rilievi ai sensi dell'articolo 6 comma 1.

(2) Classe e somiglianza sono concetti distinti: L'errore più comune tra i clienti stranieri. La somiglianza è determinata dai codici del gruppo di somiglianza, non dalla classe.

(3) Comprendere i limiti della modifica: L'ampliamento o la modifica dell'ambito dei prodotti o le modifiche sostanziali al marchio costituiscono modifiche sostanziali e vengono rifiutate.

(4) Da descrizione generica a specifica: Non è modifica sostanziale. Gli esaminatori spesso suggeriscono testo specifico di correzione.

(5) Rimedio dopo il rifiuto della modifica: Nuova domanda possibile entro 3 mesi con la data della presentazione della modifica originale.

(6) Spiegazione ai clienti stranieri: Il Giappone usa codici del gruppo di somiglianza per la valutazione della somiglianza, non solo la classe.

topic.ja: 商標権の効力：専用権・禁止権・差止請求権・損害賠償
topic.en: Trademark right effects: exclusive right, prohibition right, injunction, and damages
topic.de: Wirkungen des Markenrechts: Ausschließlichkeitsrecht, Verbotsrecht, Unterlassungsanspruch und Schadensersatz
topic.it: Effetti del diritto di marchio: diritto esclusivo, diritto di divieto, inibitoria e risarcimento danni

summary.ja: 商標権の効力は、登録商標を指定商品・役務について独占的に使用できる「専用権」と、類似商標・類似商品役務への第三者使用を禁止できる「禁止権」からなる。禁止権に基づく実際上の救済手段として差止請求権（36条）と損害賠償請求権（民法709条）がある。
summary.en: Trademark rights comprise the 'exclusive right' to use the registered mark exclusively for the designated goods or services, and the 'prohibition right' to prevent third parties from using similar marks or using marks for similar goods or services. The practical remedies arising from the prohibition right are the right to seek injunctive relief (Article 36) and the right to claim damages (Civil Code Article 709).
summary.de: Markenrechte umfassen das 'Ausschließlichkeitsrecht' zur exklusiven Nutzung der eingetragenen Marke für die designierten Waren oder Dienstleistungen und das 'Verbotsrecht', das Dritte daran hindert, ähnliche Marken zu verwenden oder Marken für ähnliche Waren oder Dienstleistungen zu nutzen. Die praktischen Rechtsmittel sind der Unterlassungsanspruch (Artikel 36) und der Schadensersatzanspruch (ZGB Artikel 709).
summary.it: I diritti di marchio comprendono il 'diritto esclusivo' di usare il marchio registrato esclusivamente per i prodotti o servizi designati e il 'diritto di divieto' per impedire a terzi di usare marchi simili o di usare marchi per prodotti o servizi simili. I rimedi pratici sono il diritto all'inibitoria (articolo 36) e il diritto al risarcimento danni (articolo 709 del Codice Civile).

law.ja:
【専用権（25条）】
商標権者は、指定商品または指定役務について登録商標の使用をする権利を専有する（25条）。ただし、専用使用権を設定したときは、専用使用権者がその登録商標の使用をする権利を専有する範囲については専用権が制限される。

専用権の効力が及ぶ範囲：登録商標と「同一」の商標を「同一」の指定商品・役務に使用する場合のみ。以下は専用権の範囲外：
①類似商標を指定商品・役務に使用する場合
②登録商標を類似商品・役務に使用する場合
③類似商標を類似商品・役務に使用する場合

専用権は商標権の本来的な効力（積極的効力）であり、商標権者が自ら商標を使用できる権利である。

【禁止権（37条1号）】
上記①②③の行為（類似範囲での使用）は専用権の範囲外であるが、商品・役務の出所混同を生じさせるおそれがあり商標権者の信用を害する。そのため、商標法は37条1号により、これらの行為を「商標権の侵害とみなす」として、差止め・損害賠償の対象としている。これが禁止権（消極的効力）。

専用権・禁止権の整理（表）：
　　　　　　｜登録商標（同一）｜類似商標
指定商品等　｜　専用権　　　　｜禁止権
類似商品等　｜　禁止権　　　　｜禁止権

禁止権の限界：禁止権はあくまで第三者の使用を禁止し得るに留まる。自己の登録商標に類似する商標（α'）を自ら使用している場合でも、その商標が第三者の別の登録商標（α''）にも類似するときは、第三者の禁止権が及ぶため、類似商標を事実上独占的に使用できるわけではない。

【専用権・禁止権の制限（29条）】
商標権の出願前に発生した特許権・意匠権・著作権等と抵触する場合、商標権の効力（専用権・禁止権）がその抵触の限度で制限される（29条）。著名なポパイマフラー事件（大阪高裁昭和60年・最高裁平成2年）では、商標出願前に発生した著作権と抵触する場合、商標権の禁止権も制限されると判断されている。

【差止請求権（36条）】
36条1項：商標権者または専用使用権者は、自己の商標権・専用使用権を侵害する者または侵害するおそれがある者に対し、その侵害の停止または予防を請求できる。
36条2項：差止請求をするに際し、侵害行為を組成した物の廃棄・侵害行為に供した設備の除却その他の侵害の予防に必要な行為を請求できる。
差止請求権は侵害者の故意・過失を要しない点が損害賠償請求権と異なる。

【損害賠償請求権（民法709条）】
商標権侵害を理由とする損害賠償請求は民法709条（不法行為）に基づく。要件：①権利侵害（商標権侵害）、②損害の発生、③故意または過失、④因果関係。

時効（民法724条）：
①短期消滅時効：被害者（商標権者）が損害及び加害者を知った時から3年（財産的損害の場合）。
②長期消滅時効：不法行為の時から20年（除斥期間）。
商標権侵害が継続している場合、侵害行為が継続する限り損害も継続して発生しているため、3年の時効は各侵害行為時から個別に進行すると解される（継続的侵害の時効）。

【損害額の算定（39条準用特許法102条）】
損害額の算定には特許法102条が準用される。①譲渡数量×商標権者の単位当たり利益額、②侵害者の利益額（推定）、③実施料相当額、の各方式が使える。
law.en:
Exclusive right (Article 25):
The trademark owner has the exclusive right to use the registered trademark in connection with the designated goods or services (Article 25). Where an exclusive license has been granted, the exclusive licensee holds the exclusive right within the licensed scope, limiting the owner's own exclusive right in that range.

Scope of the exclusive right: limited to use of the identical registered mark for identical designated goods or services. The following fall outside the exclusive right:
(1) Use of a similar mark for the designated goods or services.
(2) Use of the registered mark for similar goods or services.
(3) Use of a similar mark for similar goods or services.

The exclusive right is the inherent (positive) effect of trademark rights: the trademark owner's own right to use the mark.

Prohibition right (Article 37(i)):
Acts (1) to (3) above fall outside the exclusive right but are still liable to cause confusion as to commercial source and harm to the trademark owner's reputation. Article 37(i) therefore deems these acts to constitute trademark infringement, making them subject to injunction and damages claims. This is the prohibition right (negative effect of trademark rights).

Summary table (exclusive right / prohibition right):
                    | Identical mark | Similar mark
Designated goods    | Exclusive right | Prohibition right
Similar goods       | Prohibition right | Prohibition right

Limits of the prohibition right: The prohibition right only prevents third-party use; it does not confer an actual exclusive right to use the similar mark. Where the trademark owner itself uses a mark (α') similar to its registered mark (α), and that similar mark (α') is also similar to a third party's separately registered mark (α''), the third party's prohibition right reaches α', so the trademark owner cannot use α' freely.

Limitation of exclusive and prohibition rights (Article 29):
Where the trademark right conflicts with a patent, design right, or copyright that arose before the trademark application date, the trademark right (both exclusive and prohibition) is limited to the extent of that conflict (Article 29). In the famous Popeye Muffler case (Osaka High Court 1985; Supreme Court 1990), it was held that where the trademark conflicts with a pre-existing copyright, the trademark's prohibition right is also restricted.

Right to seek injunctive relief (Article 36):
Article 36(1): The trademark owner or exclusive licensee may demand that a person who infringes or is likely to infringe the trademark right or exclusive license cease or prevent the infringement.
Article 36(2): In making such a demand, the owner may also request destruction of articles constituting the infringing act, removal of equipment used for the infringement, and other acts necessary to prevent infringement.
Note: Unlike a damages claim, an injunction does not require the infringer's fault (intent or negligence).

Damages claim (Civil Code Article 709):
A damages claim for trademark infringement is based on Article 709 of the Civil Code (tort). Requirements: (1) infringement of a right (trademark infringement); (2) occurrence of damage; (3) intent or negligence on the part of the infringer; (4) causal connection.

Statute of limitations (Civil Code Article 724):
(1) Short-period extinctive prescription: 3 years from the time the victim (trademark owner) learned of the damage and the identity of the tortfeasor (for property damage).
(2) Long-period extinctive prescription: 20 years from the time of the tortious act (exclusionary period).
Where infringement is ongoing, damage continues to arise with each infringing act, so the 3-year prescription is generally understood to run separately from the time of each individual act of infringement.

Calculation of damages (Article 39 incorporating Patent Act Article 102):
Patent Act Article 102 is incorporated by reference for calculating damages: (1) number of units transferred by the infringer × the trademark owner's unit profit; (2) the infringer's profits (presumed to be the trademark owner's damages); or (3) a reasonable royalty amount.
law.de:
Ausschließlichkeitsrecht (Artikel 25):
Der Markeninhaber hat das ausschließliche Recht, die eingetragene Marke für die designierten Waren oder Dienstleistungen zu nutzen. Folgendes fällt außerhalb des Ausschließlichkeitsrechts: (1) ähnliche Marke für designierte Waren; (2) eingetragene Marke für ähnliche Waren; (3) ähnliche Marke für ähnliche Waren.

Verbotsrecht (Artikel 37 Nr. 1):
Die Handlungen (1) bis (3) gelten als Markenverletzung und können durch Unterlassung und Schadensersatz bekämpft werden. Das Verbotsrecht ist eine negative Wirkung des Markenrechts.

Übersichtstabelle: Designierte Waren + gleiche Marke = Ausschließlichkeitsrecht; übrige Kombinationen = Verbotsrecht.

Beschränkung (Artikel 29): Bei Konflikt mit früher entstandenen Rechten (Patent, Urheberrecht etc.) wird die Markenwirkung beschränkt (Popeye-Muffler-Fall).

Unterlassungsanspruch (Artikel 36): Kein Verschulden erforderlich. Auch Vernichtung und Beseitigung möglich.

Schadensersatz (ZGB Artikel 709): Voraussetzungen: Rechtsverletzung, Schaden, Verschulden, Kausalität. Verjährung: 3 Jahre ab Kenntnis von Schaden und Täter; 20 Jahre ab Tatzeit. Schadensberechnung: Artikel 102 PatG analog.
law.it:
Diritto esclusivo (articolo 25):
Il titolare del marchio ha il diritto esclusivo di usare il marchio registrato per i prodotti o servizi designati. Fuori dall'ambito esclusivo: (1) marchio simile per prodotti designati; (2) marchio registrato per prodotti simili; (3) marchio simile per prodotti simili.

Diritto di divieto (articolo 37 n. 1):
Gli atti (1)-(3) sono considerati violazione del marchio e possono essere contrastati con inibitoria e risarcimento. Il diritto di divieto è un effetto negativo del diritto di marchio.

Tabella riassuntiva: Prodotti designati + marchio identico = diritto esclusivo; altre combinazioni = diritto di divieto.

Limitazione (articolo 29): In caso di conflitto con diritti anteriori (brevetti, diritti d'autore), l'efficacia del marchio è limitata (caso Popeye Muffler).

Inibitoria (articolo 36): Non richiede colpa dell'autore della violazione. Possibile anche distruzione e rimozione.

Risarcimento danni (articolo 709 c.c.): Requisiti: violazione, danno, colpa, nesso causale. Prescrizione: 3 anni dalla conoscenza del danno e dell'autore; 20 anni dal fatto illecito. Calcolo: articolo 102 Legge Brevetti per analogia.

practice.ja:
専用権・禁止権・差止請求権・損害賠償請求権の実務上のポイントは以下のとおり。

①専用権と禁止権の区別を海外取引先に明確に伝える：「商標権＝同一商標・同一商品の独占使用権のみ」という誤解をよく見かける。実際には類似の範囲まで（禁止権の範囲で）差止め・損害賠償の対象となることを説明する。

②差止請求は故意・過失不要：侵害の事実だけで差止請求が可能。これは損害賠償との最大の違いであり、侵害発見時には迅速に差止請求を行うことが実務上重要。

③損害賠償の時効管理が最重要：3年の短期消滅時効は「損害および加害者を知った時」から進行。商標権侵害が長期間継続している場合、知った時点から3年以上前の侵害行為については時効が完成している可能性がある。調査・証拠収集と並行して、時効の進行を止める手段（裁判上の請求・内容証明郵便等）を速やかに講じることが重要。

④継続的侵害の時効計算：継続的な侵害行為の場合、各侵害行為（各販売行為等）が個別の不法行為として時効が進行するという考え方が一般的。「侵害の事実を3年以上前に知っていた」という抗弁が認められる可能性があるため、侵害発見後は速やかに行動することが不可欠。

⑤損害額の算定方式の選択：特許法102条の準用により3つの算定方式（①実施料相当額・②侵害者利益・③商標権者の損害額）が使えるが、実務的には証明しやすい方式を選択する。輸入品・模倣品など証拠入手が困難なケースでは実施料相当額方式が有用。

⑥海外権利者への説明：日本の差止請求権（36条）はドイツ法や英米法のinjunctionに対応。損害賠償請求は民法709条の不法行為。時効制度（3年・20年）も説明しておくと、海外弁護士・代理人との意思疎通がスムーズになる。ポパイマフラー事件の判例は、先行著作権が存在する場合に商標権の行使が制限される場合があることを示す重要判例として説明する価値がある。
practice.en:
Key practical points on exclusive right, prohibition right, injunction, and damages:

(1) Clearly explain to overseas clients the distinction between exclusive and prohibition rights: A common misunderstanding is that 'trademark rights = only the exclusive right to use the identical mark for identical goods.' In practice, the prohibition right extends protection to similar marks and similar goods or services, making injunction and damages claims available across that broader range.

(2) No fault required for injunction: An injunction under Article 36 can be sought based on the fact of infringement alone, without needing to prove intent or negligence. This is the key distinction from a damages claim. Prompt pursuit of injunctive relief upon discovery of infringement is important in practice.

(3) Statute of limitations management for damages is critical: The 3-year short-period extinctive prescription runs from the time the trademark owner learned of the damage and the infringer's identity. Where infringement has continued for a long period, claims for acts of infringement that occurred more than 3 years before the owner learned of them may be time-barred. Steps to interrupt the prescription (court proceedings, certified letter, etc.) should be taken promptly alongside investigation and evidence collection.

(4) Prescription calculation for ongoing infringement: For continuous infringement, each infringing act (each sale, etc.) is generally treated as a separate tort from which the prescription runs independently. The risk that the infringer may raise a time-bar defense for acts occurring more than 3 years before the owner's knowledge makes prompt action essential.

(5) Choosing a damages calculation method: Three methods are available under Patent Act Article 102 as incorporated by reference: (1) reasonable royalty; (2) infringer's profits; (3) trademark owner's actual damages. In practice, choose the most provable method. Where evidence is difficult to obtain (e.g., imported counterfeit goods), the reasonable royalty method is often the most practical.

(6) Explaining to overseas clients: Japan's injunction right (Article 36) corresponds to the injunction in German and common law systems. Damages are based on Article 709 of the Civil Code (tort). Explaining the prescription periods (3 years/20 years) helps communication with overseas counsel. The Popeye Muffler case is an important precedent to mention where the client holds pre-existing intellectual property rights that may conflict with the trademark right they seek to enforce.
practice.de:
Wichtige praktische Punkte:

(1) Unterschied zwischen Ausschließlichkeits- und Verbotsrecht klar erläutern: Häufiges Missverständnis: Markenrecht = nur Ausschließlichkeitsrecht für identische Marke/Waren. Das Verbotsrecht erstreckt sich auf ähnliche Marken/Waren.

(2) Kein Verschulden für Unterlassung erforderlich: Unterlassungsanspruch ist allein durch die Verletzungstatsache begründet. Sofortige Geltendmachung bei Entdeckung empfohlen.

(3) Verjährungsmanagement für Schadensersatz ist entscheidend: 3-Jahres-Frist läuft ab Kenntnis von Schaden und Täter. Bei langanhaltender Verletzung können frühere Handlungen verjährt sein.

(4) Verjährung bei fortdauernder Verletzung: Jede Verletzungshandlung läuft separat. Sofortiges Handeln nach Entdeckung unerlässlich.

(5) Wahl der Schadensberechnungsmethode: Drei Methoden nach Artikel 102 PatG analog. Bei Beweisschwierigkeiten: Lizenzgebühr-Methode.

(6) Erklärung gegenüber ausländischen Mandanten: Artikel 36 entspricht der Injunction. Artikel 709 ZGB entspricht dem Tort. Popeye-Muffler-Fall als wichtiges Präzedenz erwähnen.
practice.it:
Punti pratici chiave:

(1) Spiegare chiaramente la distinzione tra diritto esclusivo e di divieto: Malinteso comune: diritto di marchio = solo diritto esclusivo per marchio/prodotti identici. Il diritto di divieto si estende a marchi/prodotti simili.

(2) Nessuna colpa richiesta per l'inibitoria: L'inibitoria è fondata solo sul fatto della violazione. Azione immediata alla scoperta raccomandata.

(3) Gestione della prescrizione per danni è critica: 3 anni dalla conoscenza del danno e dell'autore. Per violazioni prolungate, atti precedenti potrebbero essere prescritti.

(4) Prescrizione per violazioni continue: Ogni atto di violazione decorre separatamente. Azione immediata dopo la scoperta essenziale.

(5) Scelta del metodo di calcolo danni: Tre metodi ai sensi dell'articolo 102 Legge Brevetti per analogia. Per prove difficili: metodo del canone ragionevole.

(6) Spiegazione ai clienti stranieri: Articolo 36 corrisponde all'injunction. Articolo 709 c.c. corrisponde al tort. Caso Popeye Muffler come precedente importante da menzionare.

topic.ja: 商標権侵害における損害額の算定（38条）
topic.en: Calculation of damages for trademark infringement (Article 38)
topic.it: Calcolo del risarcimento danni per violazione del marchio (articolo 38)

summary.ja: 商標権侵害による損害の立証は困難なため、商標法38条は①譲渡数量×商標権者の単位利益額、②侵害者の利益額（推定）、③ライセンス料相当額、④商標権取得・維持費用相当額の4つの算定方式を定める。いずれも損害の発生自体は原則として別途立証が必要（ライセンス料は一応の推定あり）。
summary.en: Because proving damages from trademark infringement is difficult, Article 38 provides four calculation methods: (1) units transferred by the infringer × the trademark owner's unit profit; (2) the infringer's profits (presumed to be the owner's damages); (3) a reasonable royalty; and (4) costs of acquiring and maintaining the trademark right. In each case, the occurrence of damage itself must in principle be proven separately (with a rebuttable presumption for the royalty method).
summary.de: Da der Nachweis von Schäden aus Markenverletzungen schwierig ist, sieht Artikel 38 vier Berechnungsmethoden vor: (1) übertragene Einheiten × Gewinn des Inhabers je Einheit; (2) Gewinn des Verletzers (als Schaden des Inhabers vermutet); (3) angemessene Lizenzgebühr; und (4) Kosten für Erwerb und Aufrechterhaltung des Markenrechts. In jedem Fall muss der Schadenseintritt selbst grundsätzlich gesondert nachgewiesen werden (mit widerlegbarer Vermutung bei der Lizenzmethode).
summary.it: Poiché è difficile provare i danni derivanti dalla violazione del marchio, l'articolo 38 prevede quattro metodi di calcolo: (1) unità trasferite dall'autore della violazione × utile per unità del titolare; (2) profitti dell'autore della violazione (presunti come danni del titolare); (3) canone ragionevole; e (4) costi di acquisizione e mantenimento del diritto di marchio. In ogni caso, il verificarsi del danno deve essere in linea di principio provato separatamente (con presunzione relativa per il metodo del canone).

law.ja:
【損害立証の困難性と38条の趣旨】
不法行為に基づく損害賠償は本来「侵害がなかった場合との財産状態の差額」（差額説）で算定するが、商標権侵害の場合、侵害がなければどれだけ売れていたかを立証することは極めて困難。そのため38条は立証の困難性を緩和する4つの算定方式を定める。

【38条1項：譲渡数量×単位利益額方式】
①号（逸失利益型）：商標権者の単位数量当たりの利益の額×侵害者の譲渡数量（ただし商標権者の使用相応数量を上限とし、商標権者が販売できない事情に相当する特定数量を控除）。
②号（ライセンス料補完型）：使用相応数量を超える数量または特定数量がある場合、それらに応じた登録商標の使用に対し受けるべき金銭の額に相当する額（ただし商標権者がライセンスをし得ないと認められる場合を除く）。
→1項は①②の合計額を損害額とすることができる。

【38条2項：侵害者利益額の推定】
侵害者がその侵害行為により利益を受けているときは、その利益の額を商標権者の損害額と推定する。「利益」とは粗利・経常利益ではなく、限界利益（売上から変動費を控除したもの）と解されている。推定規定のため侵害者が反証することで覆すことが可能。

【38条3項：ライセンス料相当額】
商標権者は、登録商標の使用に対し受けるべき金銭の額（ライセンス料相当額）を損害額として請求できる。通常は侵害品の売上額×ライセンス料率で算定。6項により3項の額を超える損害賠償の請求を妨げない（最低限度額として機能）。

【38条4項：ライセンス料相当額認定の考慮事項】
1項2号・3項のライセンス料相当額を認定する際、裁判所は「侵害があったことを前提として侵害者との間で合意をするとしたならば得ることとなる対価」を考慮できる（いわゆるエクス・アンテ型ライセンス料の考慮）。これにより、侵害後の事後的な交渉で合意されるであろう実施料（侵害前の通常実施料より高額になりうる）を考慮した認定が可能。

【38条5項：商標権取得・維持費用相当額】
侵害が指定商品・役務についての登録商標（社会通念上同一と認められる商標を含む）の使用によるものであるときは、商標権の取得・維持に通常要する費用相当額を損害額とすることができる。出願費用・代理人費用・更新費用等が対象。侵害行為を立証するだけで一定額を請求できる点で実用的。6項により5項の額を超える損害賠償の請求を妨げない。

【38条6項：故意・重大過失の参酌】
3項・5項は損害賠償の最低限度額として機能する。ただしこれを超える損害の請求を妨げない。この場合、侵害者に故意・重大過失がなかったときは、裁判所は損害額を定めるにあたりこれを参酌（減額考慮）できる。

【損害の発生と競合関係の要件】
2項（侵害者利益）および1項（逸失利益）の方式では、損害の発生は推定されないため、商標権者は損害の発生を立証する必要がある。商標権者と侵害者の商品・役務が競合関係にない場合（例：地理的に需要者が重ならない飲食店）は逸失利益が生じない（舞豚事件・東京地裁令和3年4月23日判決）。3項（ライセンス料）については、標章の使用が売上に全く貢献していない場合は損害不発生とされるが（最判平成9年3月11日・小僧寿し事件）、損害発生の一応の推定があり、貢献していないことの反証は侵害者側が行う。
law.en:
Difficulty of proving damages and the purpose of Article 38:
Damages for tort are in principle calculated as the difference in financial position between the actual situation and the hypothetical situation without the infringement (difference theory). In trademark cases, proving how much more the owner would have sold without the infringement is extremely difficult. Article 38 therefore provides four methods to ease this burden.

Article 38(1): Units transferred × unit profit method:
Item (i) (lost profit type): The trademark owner's unit profit × the number of units transferred by the infringer, capped at the number of units the owner had the capacity to supply, with a deduction for any units the owner would not have been able to sell regardless.
Item (ii) (supplementary royalty type): For units exceeding the owner's supply capacity or units the owner could not have sold, the amount equivalent to the royalty receivable for those units (unless the owner would not have been able to grant a license).
The sum of (i) and (ii) may be claimed as damages under paragraph 1.

Article 38(2): Presumption of infringer's profits:
Where the infringer has made profits from the infringing acts, those profits are presumed to equal the trademark owner's damages. 'Profits' here means marginal profit (sales less variable costs), not gross or operating profit. The presumption may be rebutted by the infringer.

Article 38(3): Reasonable royalty:
The trademark owner may claim the amount equivalent to the reasonable royalty for use of the registered trademark as its damages. Typically calculated as infringing sales × royalty rate. Article 38(6) provides that paragraph (3) does not bar claims for damages exceeding this amount (it functions as a floor).

Article 38(4): Factors for determining reasonable royalty:
In determining the royalty amount under paragraphs (1)(ii) and (3), the court may take into account what royalty the parties would have agreed on in a negotiation conducted on the assumption that the infringement had occurred (ex ante infringement royalty). This allows recognition of a higher royalty than the pre-infringement standard licensing rate.

Article 38(5): Costs of acquiring and maintaining the trademark right:
Where the infringement involves use of the registered trademark (including marks socially equivalent to the registered mark) for designated goods or services, the ordinary costs of acquiring and maintaining the trademark right may be claimed as damages. This covers application fees, agent fees, renewal fees, etc. It is practically useful as a minimum amount claimable by proving the infringing use alone. Article 38(6) does not bar claims for damages exceeding this amount.

Article 38(6): Consideration of intent/gross negligence:
Paragraphs (3) and (5) function as minimum damage amounts. Claims for higher damages are not barred. However, where the infringer lacked intent or gross negligence, the court may take this into account (as grounds for reduction) when determining damages.

Occurrence of damage and competition requirement:
Methods under paragraphs (1) and (2) do not presume the occurrence of damage, so the trademark owner must prove it. Where the parties are not in competition (e.g., geographically separate restaurants with non-overlapping customers), no lost profits arise (Maitton case, Tokyo District Court, April 23, 2021). For the royalty method under paragraph (3), damage is presumed unless the infringer proves the mark's use made no contribution to sales (Kozozushi case, Supreme Court, March 11, 1997).
law.de:
Schwierigkeit des Schadensbeweises und Zweck von Artikel 38:
Im Markenrecht ist es extrem schwierig zu beweisen, wie viel der Inhaber ohne die Verletzung verkauft hätte. Artikel 38 sieht daher vier Berechnungsmethoden vor.

Artikel 38 Abs. 1 (Einheiten × Gewinn-Methode): Gewinn des Inhabers je Einheit × übertragene Einheiten (bis zur Kapazitätsgrenze, mit Abzug nicht verkaufbarer Einheiten). Ergänzend Lizenzbetrag für Einheiten über der Kapazitätsgrenze.

Artikel 38 Abs. 2 (Gewinn des Verletzers): Gewinn des Verletzers als Schaden des Inhabers vermutet. 'Gewinn' = Deckungsbeitrag (Umsatz abzüglich variabler Kosten). Vermutung widerlegbar.

Artikel 38 Abs. 3 (Angemessene Lizenzgebühr): Mindestbetrag des Schadensersatzes. Berechnung: Umsatz der Verletzungsprodukte × Lizenzrate. Abs. 6 erlaubt höhere Forderungen.

Artikel 38 Abs. 4 (Ex-ante-Lizenzgebühr): Gericht kann hypothetische Verhandlungsgebühr berücksichtigen.

Artikel 38 Abs. 5 (Erwerbskosten): Erwerbs- und Aufrechterhaltungskosten als Mindestschaden. Abs. 6 erlaubt höhere Forderungen.

Schadenseintritt und Wettbewerbserfordernis: Methoden nach Abs. 1 und 2 setzen Nachweis des Schadenseintritts voraus. Bei fehlender Wettbewerbsbeziehung kein entgangener Gewinn.
law.it:
Difficoltà della prova e scopo dell'articolo 38:
Nei casi di marchio, è estremamente difficile provare quanto il titolare avrebbe venduto senza la violazione. L'articolo 38 prevede quindi quattro metodi.

Articolo 38 comma 1 (Metodo unità × utile): Utile per unità del titolare × unità trasferite (fino alla capacità del titolare, con deduzione delle unità non vendibili). Complementariamente, canone per unità eccedenti la capacità.

Articolo 38 comma 2 (Profitti del violatore): I profitti del violatore sono presunti come danni del titolare. 'Profitti' = margine di contribuzione. La presunzione è confutabile.

Articolo 38 comma 3 (Canone ragionevole): Importo minimo del risarcimento. Calcolo: fatturato dei prodotti contraffatti × tasso di canone. Comma 6 consente richieste superiori.

Articolo 38 comma 4 (Canone ex-ante): Il tribunale può considerare il canone che le parti avrebbero negoziato assumendo la violazione.

Articolo 38 comma 5 (Costi di acquisizione): Costi di acquisizione e mantenimento come danno minimo. Comma 6 consente richieste superiori.

Verificazione del danno e requisito di concorrenza: I metodi dei commi 1 e 2 richiedono la prova del danno. In assenza di rapporto di concorrenza, nessun profitto perso.

practice.ja:
損害額算定の実務上のポイントは以下のとおり。

①算定方式の選択が戦略の核心：商標権者は3つ（または4つ）の算定方式を事案に応じて選択できる。侵害者の帳簿・売上データが入手できる場合は①または②が有利。競合関係のない侵害（例：地域が異なる同業者）では①②は認められにくく③（ライセンス料）が現実的。侵害規模が小さい場合は⑤（取得・維持費用）が実用的な最低保障として機能する。

②競合関係の有無が最重要の前提：逸失利益（1項）・侵害者利益（2項）を損害とするためには、商標権者と侵害者の商品・役務が競合関係にあることが必要。舞豚事件（東京地裁令和3年）のように、地理的に需要者が重ならない場合は競合関係なしとして損害不発生が認められる場合がある。事前に競合関係の有無を分析しておくことが重要。

③ライセンス料率の設定：3項の実施料相当額は通常「売上×料率」で算定されるが、料率の決定が争点となることが多い。業界相場・過去のライセンス事例・侵害の悪質性等が考慮される。4項（エクス・アンテ型）の導入により、侵害があったことを前提とした高めの料率が認定される可能性も生まれた。舞豚事件では業界平均より高い8%が認定された。

④38条5項の活用：商標権の取得・維持費用相当額は証明が容易（出願費用・維持費の領収書等）。侵害規模が小さく損害額の立証が困難な場合や、侵害者との競合関係が不明確な場合の最低限度額として有用。ただし実際の損害が費用相当額を大きく上回る場合は他の方式と併用を検討する。

⑤海外権利者への説明：日本の38条は特許法102条の準用に相当するものを商標法に直接規定したもの。EU・米国のdamages regimeと共通点が多い（逸失利益・不当利得・ライセンス料相当の三類型）が、4項のエクス・アンテ型ライセンス料考慮は日本独自の立法例として説明する価値がある。また、5項の取得・維持費用相当額算定方式も日本独自の規定として紹介できる。
practice.en:
Key practical points on calculating damages:

(1) Choosing the calculation method is the strategic core: The trademark owner may choose among three (or four) methods depending on the case. Where the infringer's accounts and sales data are accessible, methods (1) or (2) may be advantageous. Where the parties are not in competition (e.g., similar businesses in different regions), methods (1) and (2) are difficult to establish and the royalty method (3) is more realistic. Where the scale of infringement is small, the costs method (5) functions as a practical minimum guarantee.

(2) Whether the parties compete is the most critical preliminary issue: Claiming lost profits (paragraph 1) or infringer's profits (paragraph 2) requires a competitive relationship. As in the Maitton case (Tokyo District Court, 2021), where customers do not overlap geographically, no competitive relationship may be found and therefore no lost profit damage. Analyzing the competitive relationship in advance is essential.

(3) Setting the royalty rate: The paragraph (3) royalty amount is typically calculated as sales × rate, but the rate is often contested. Industry norms, prior licensing agreements, and the severity of the infringement are considered. The introduction of paragraph (4) (ex ante approach) means a higher rate—based on a notional negotiation against the background of the infringement—may be recognized. In the Maitton case, 8% (higher than industry average) was recognized.

(4) Using Article 38(5): The costs of acquiring and maintaining the trademark right are relatively easy to prove (receipts for application fees, maintenance fees, etc.). It is useful as a minimum amount where the infringement scale is small, damages are hard to prove, or the competitive relationship is unclear. Where actual damages significantly exceed this amount, consider combining with another method.

(5) Explaining to overseas clients: Japan's Article 38 provides directly in the Trademark Act what is incorporated by reference from Patent Act Article 102 in the patent context. It shares much with EU and US damages regimes (lost profits, unjust enrichment, reasonable royalty), but the paragraph (4) ex ante royalty consideration and the paragraph (5) acquisition/maintenance cost method are distinctive Japanese provisions worth highlighting.
practice.de:
Wichtige praktische Punkte zur Schadensberechnung:

(1) Die Wahl der Berechnungsmethode ist der strategische Kern. Bei zugänglichen Daten des Verletzers: Methoden (1) oder (2). Bei fehlender Wettbewerbsbeziehung: Lizenzmethode (3). Bei kleiner Verletzung: Kostenmethode (5) als Mindestgarantie.

(2) Das Wettbewerbsverhältnis ist die wichtigste Vorfrage. Entgangener Gewinn und Verletzungsgewinn erfordern eine Wettbewerbsbeziehung (Maitton-Fall). Vorabanalyse entscheidend.

(3) Festlegung des Lizenzsatzes: Branchenübliche Rate, frühere Lizenzen, Schwere der Verletzung. Abs. 4 (Ex-ante) ermöglicht höhere Anerkennung. Im Maitton-Fall 8% anerkannt.

(4) Nutzung von Artikel 38 Abs. 5: Einfach zu beweisen. Sinnvoll bei kleinen Verletzungen oder unklarer Wettbewerbsbeziehung als Mindestbetrag.

(5) Erklärung gegenüber ausländischen Mandanten: Artikel 38 entspricht dem Schutzsystem von EU und USA. Abs. 4 (Ex-ante) und Abs. 5 (Kostenersatz) sind japanische Besonderheiten.
practice.it:
Punti pratici chiave sul calcolo dei danni:

(1) La scelta del metodo di calcolo è il nucleo strategico. Con dati del violatore disponibili: metodi (1) o (2). Senza rapporto di concorrenza: metodo del canone (3). Per violazioni piccole: metodo dei costi (5) come garanzia minima.

(2) Il rapporto di concorrenza è la questione preliminare più critica. Profitto perso e profitti del violatore richiedono un rapporto di concorrenza (caso Maitton). Analisi preliminare essenziale.

(3) Determinazione del tasso di canone: Norma di settore, licenze precedenti, gravità della violazione. Comma 4 (ex-ante) può consentire un riconoscimento più alto. Nel caso Maitton riconosciuto l'8%.

(4) Utilizzo dell'articolo 38 comma 5: Facile da provare. Utile per violazioni piccole o rapporto di concorrenza poco chiaro come importo minimo.

(5) Spiegazione ai clienti stranieri: Articolo 38 corrisponde ai regimi di danni UE e USA. Comma 4 (ex-ante) e comma 5 (rimborso costi) sono caratteristiche specificamente giapponesi.

topic.ja: 並行輸入と商標権侵害：フレッドペリー3要件
topic.en: Parallel imports and trademark infringement: the Fred Perry three-part test
topic.de: Parallelimporte und Markenverletzung: der dreiteilige Fred-Perry-Test
topic.it: Importazioni parallele e violazione del marchio: il test in tre parti Fred Perry

summary.ja: 真正品の並行輸入は形式的には商標権侵害に当たりうるが、フレッドペリー判決（最高裁平成15年）の3要件を満たす場合は実質的違法性を欠き、商標権侵害に当たらない。3要件は①適法な商標付与、②同一出所、③実質的品質同等性。
summary.en: Parallel importation of genuine goods may formally constitute trademark infringement, but where the three requirements established in the Fred Perry case (Supreme Court 2003) are met, the act lacks substantial illegality and does not constitute trademark infringement. The three requirements are: (1) lawful affixation of the mark; (2) same commercial origin; and (3) no material difference in quality.
summary.de: Parallelimporte echter Waren können formal eine Markenverletzung darstellen, aber wenn die drei Voraussetzungen aus dem Fred-Perry-Fall (Oberster Gerichtshof 2003) erfüllt sind, fehlt es an wesentlicher Rechtswidrigkeit. Die drei Voraussetzungen sind: (1) rechtmäßige Anbringung der Marke; (2) gleiche Herkunft; (3) keine wesentlichen Qualitätsunterschiede.
summary.it: L'importazione parallela di beni genuini può formalmente costituire violazione del marchio, ma quando sono soddisfatti i tre requisiti stabiliti nel caso Fred Perry (Corte Suprema 2003), l'atto manca di illegalità sostanziale. I tre requisiti sono: (1) apposizione legale del marchio; (2) stessa origine commerciale; (3) nessuna differenza sostanziale di qualità.

law.ja:
【並行輸入とは・形式的侵害性】
商標が付された商品を商標権者の許諾なく輸入する行為は、商標法2条3項2号（輸入行為も商標の使用に該当）および25条（商標権者の専用権）により、形式的には商標権侵害に当たる。しかし、海外で商標権者または正規ライセンシーが正規に製造・販売した真正品を輸入する行為（並行輸入）は、商標の出所表示機能・品質保証機能を害しない場合がある。

【パーカー事件（大阪地裁昭和45年）】
並行輸入の適法性を最初に認めた先駆的判例。商標法は出所表示・品質保証機能の保護を目的とし、真正品の並行輸入ではこれらの機能が害されないとして商標権侵害を否定。

【フレッドペリー並行輸入事件（最小判平成15年2月27日）】
最高裁が並行輸入の適法性要件を3要件として明示した。3要件をすべて満たす場合は「実質的違法性を欠く」として商標権侵害に当たらない。

第1要件：商標が外国における商標権者または当該商標権者から使用許諾を受けた者により「適法に」付されたこと。
第2要件：外国における商標権者と日本の商標権者とが同一人であるか、または法律的・経済的に同一人と同視し得るような関係があること（同一の出所を表示するものであること）。
第3要件：日本の商標権者が直接的または間接的に当該商品の品質管理を行いうる立場にあることから、当該商品と日本の商標権者が登録商標を付した商品とが当該登録商標の保証する品質において実質的に差異がないこと。

【フレッドペリー事件における3要件非充足の判断】
本件では、ライセンシーが許諾地域外の中国工場に下請製造させた商品が問題となった。許諾地域外での「製造行為」はそもそも商標を適法に付する権限を欠くとして第1要件が否定され、商標権者の品質管理が及ばないとして第3要件も否定された。

【知財高裁令和3年5月19日判決（令和2(ネ)10062）との比較】
本件では、代理店契約解除後にかつ地域制限条項に違反して商品を販売した代理店から購入した商品の並行輸入が問題となった。裁判所は3要件を充足するとして並行輸入の適法性を認めた。

第1要件：代理店契約の解除や地域制限条項違反は「販売行為」の問題にすぎず、商品そのものは商標権者が製造した真正品。商標が適法に付されたかどうかは、「商標を付した行為」の適法性を問うものであり、その後の流通上の契約違反は影響しない（債権的効果にすぎない）。フレッドペリー事件と異なるのは、同事件では許諾地域外での「製造行為」（商標付与行為）が問題となったのに対し、本件では真正品の「販売行為」の問題にすぎない点。

第2要件：海外・日本いずれも同一の商標権者であるため問題なく充足。

第3要件：商標権者自身が製造した真正品であり、経年劣化等の品質問題もなく充足。

【並行輸入と販売地域制限の限界】
代理店への地域制限条項は商標権者と代理店との間の債権的義務にすぎない。契約に拘束されない第三者（並行輸入業者）との関係では、地域制限違反を理由に並行輸入を商標権侵害として阻止することはできない。並行輸入を阻止するには、3要件のいずれかを充足しないような実質的な事情（出所・品質の差異）が必要。
law.en:
Parallel imports and formal infringement:
Importing goods bearing a trademark without the trademark owner's authorization formally falls under the definition of trademark use (Article 2(3)(ii)—importation is included) and the exclusive right (Article 25). However, importing genuine goods that were lawfully manufactured and sold abroad by the trademark owner or a licensed party (parallel importation) may not harm the source-indicating or quality-guarantee functions of the trademark.

Parker case (Osaka District Court, 1970):
The pioneering decision recognizing the legality of parallel imports. Held that trademark law protects source-indicating and quality-guarantee functions, and that parallel importation of genuine goods does not harm these functions, and therefore does not constitute trademark infringement.

Fred Perry parallel import case (Supreme Court, February 27, 2003):
The Supreme Court expressly formulated three requirements for the legality of parallel imports. Where all three are met, the act 'lacks substantial illegality' and does not constitute trademark infringement.

Requirement 1: The trademark was 'lawfully' affixed by the trademark owner in the foreign country or by a person who received a license from that trademark owner.
Requirement 2: The foreign trademark owner and the Japanese trademark owner are the same person, or stand in a relationship such that they can be treated as legally or economically equivalent, so that the trademark indicates the same commercial origin as the Japanese registered trademark.
Requirement 3: Because the Japanese trademark owner is in a position to manage the quality of the goods directly or indirectly, the goods and the goods to which the Japanese trademark owner has affixed the registered trademark are not materially different in the quality guaranteed by that trademark.

Why the three requirements were not met in the Fred Perry case itself:
The goods in that case had been manufactured by a licensee at a factory in China outside the licensed territory—a manufacturing act (the act of affixing the mark) for which the licensee lacked authority. Requirement 1 was therefore not met. Requirement 3 was also not met because the trademark owner could not exercise quality control.

Comparison with IP High Court decision, May 19, 2021 (Case 2020(Ne)10062):
This case involved parallel importation of goods purchased from a former dealer after cancellation of the dealer agreement, and in breach of a territorial restriction clause. The court held all three requirements met and found the parallel import lawful.

Requirement 1: The cancellation of the dealer agreement and the territorial restriction breach were matters of 'sales conduct' only. The goods themselves were genuine goods manufactured by the trademark owner. Whether the trademark was 'lawfully affixed' asks only about the act of affixing the mark, not about subsequent contractual breaches in distribution (which have only contractual, not proprietary, effect). The distinction from the Fred Perry case is that Fred Perry involved a manufacturing act (affixing the mark) outside the licensed territory, whereas this case involved only a sales act concerning genuine goods.

Requirement 2: Met without issue as the same entity held the marks in both jurisdictions.

Requirement 3: Met, as the goods were genuine goods manufactured by the trademark owner itself, with no quality degradation issues.

Limits of territorial restrictions in dealer agreements:
Territorial restrictions in dealer agreements create contractual obligations only between the trademark owner and the dealer; they do not bind third parties such as parallel importers who are not parties to the agreement. To block parallel imports based on trademark rights, a substantive circumstance must exist that causes one of the three requirements to fail—i.e., a genuine difference in origin or quality.
law.de:
Parallelimporte und formale Verletzung:
Der Import von mit einer Marke versehenen Waren ohne Genehmigung des Inhabers stellt formal eine Nutzung der Marke dar. Bei echten Waren, die vom Inhaber oder einem Lizenznehmer im Ausland rechtmäßig hergestellt und verkauft wurden, können Herkunfts- und Qualitätsfunktionen der Marke jedoch unbeeinträchtigt bleiben.

Parker-Fall (1970): Bahnbrechende Entscheidung, die die Rechtmäßigkeit von Parallelimporten anerkannte.

Fred-Perry-Fall (Oberster Gerichtshof, 2003): Drei Voraussetzungen für rechtmäßige Parallelimporte:
(1) Rechtmäßige Anbringung der Marke durch den ausländischen Inhaber oder Lizenznehmer.
(2) Gleiche oder wirtschaftlich gleichwertige Identität zwischen ausländischem und inländischem Inhaber.
(3) Keine wesentlichen Qualitätsunterschiede durch direkte oder indirekte Qualitätskontrolle des inländischen Inhabers.

Im Fred-Perry-Fall selbst wurden die Voraussetzungen nicht erfüllt, da der Lizenznehmer außerhalb der genehmigten Region produziert hatte.

IP High Court 2021: Gebietseinschränkungen in Händlerverträgen haben nur schuldrechtliche Wirkung. Parallelimporte von echten Waren durch Dritte können nicht mit dem Markenrecht blockiert werden.

Grenzen der Gebietseinschränkungen: Nur bei tatsächlichen Unterschieden in Herkunft oder Qualität können Parallelimporte blockiert werden.
law.it:
Importazioni parallele e violazione formale:
L'importazione di beni con marchio senza autorizzazione del titolare è formalmente un uso del marchio. Tuttavia, per beni genuini fabbricati e venduti all'estero dal titolare o da un licenziatario, le funzioni di indicazione dell'origine e di garanzia della qualità possono non essere danneggiate.

Caso Parker (1970): Decisione pionieristica che riconobbe la legittimità delle importazioni parallele.

Caso Fred Perry (Corte Suprema, 2003): Tre requisiti per le importazioni parallele legittime:
(1) Apposizione legale del marchio da parte del titolare straniero o del licenziatario.
(2) Identità o equivalenza economica tra titolare straniero e titolare giapponese.
(3) Nessuna differenza sostanziale di qualità grazie al controllo diretto o indiretto della qualità da parte del titolare giapponese.

Nel caso Fred Perry stesso, i requisiti non erano soddisfatti perché il licenziatario aveva prodotto fuori dalla regione autorizzata.

IP High Court 2021: Le restrizioni territoriali nei contratti di distribuzione hanno solo effetti obbligatori. I terzi importatori paralleli di beni genuini non possono essere bloccati con diritti di marchio.

Limiti delle restrizioni territoriali: Solo differenze sostanziali di origine o qualità possono bloccare le importazioni parallele.

practice.ja:
並行輸入の実務上のポイントは以下のとおり。

①海外権利者の最重要関心事：海外ブランドにとって並行輸入問題は市場の価格統制・ブランド管理の核心的課題。日本の3要件法理（フレッドペリー判例）を正確に説明することが海外代理人・権利者との実務上の最重要事項の一つ。

②3要件の実務的チェックポイント：
第1要件の検討：問題となる商品は誰が製造したか、商標を付した行為は商標権者またはライセンシーが許諾の範囲内で行ったか。「製造行為」の適法性が核心（販売行為の適法性は直接影響しない）。
第2要件の検討：海外と日本の商標権者の同一性・経済的同一視可能性を確認。グループ会社関係、ライセンス関係等を整理。
第3要件の検討：品質管理の有無と実質的品質差異の有無。並行輸入品と日本正規品の間に品質差がある場合（劣化・地域仕様の差異・アフターサービスの差等）は3要件不充足となりうる。

③販売地域制限だけでは並行輸入を阻止できない：代理店契約の地域制限条項は債権的効果にすぎず、契約外の第三者に対して商標権侵害を主張することはできない（知財高裁令和3年判決）。並行輸入を阻止したい場合は、①製造段階での管理強化（許諾地域外での製造禁止の担保）、または②真正品と国内正規品の間に実質的な品質差・仕様差を設けることが唯一の現実的な手段。

④内外価格差と消費者利益：並行輸入問題の根源は内外価格差。商標権を用いて内外価格差を維持するには、出所・品質の実質的差異が必要であり、それはむしろ並行輸入品の魅力を損なう方向に働く。商標権は内外価格政策を守るツールとして機能しないことを海外権利者に明確に伝えることが重要。

⑤EU・米国との比較：EUでは商標の消尽原則（EEA域内で真正品を市場に置いた場合の消尽）があり、域内並行輸入は原則として商標権侵害を構成しない。米国ではLevi Strauss v. Costco等の判例があり、地域差・品質差が重要な争点となる。日本の3要件法理はEU・米国と概念的に共通する部分が多く、「品質保証機能・出所表示機能を害さない限り適法」という原則として説明できる。
practice.en:
Key practical points on parallel imports:

(1) The top concern for overseas rights holders: Parallel import issues are central to price control and brand management for overseas brands in the Japanese market. Accurately explaining Japan's three-requirement doctrine (Fred Perry precedent) is one of the most important practical tasks in communications with overseas counsel and rights holders.

(2) Practical checklist for the three requirements:
Requirement 1: Who manufactured the goods in question? Was the act of affixing the trademark performed by the trademark owner or a licensee within the scope of their authorization? The legality of the manufacturing act is the core issue (the legality of subsequent sales conduct has no direct impact).
Requirement 2: Confirm the identity or economic equivalence between the foreign and Japanese trademark owners. Map out group company relationships and licensing structures.
Requirement 3: Assess whether quality control is exercised and whether any material quality difference exists. Where there is a quality difference between the parallel import and the domestic genuine product (deterioration, regional specifications, after-service differences, etc.), Requirement 3 may not be met.

(3) Territorial restrictions in dealer agreements cannot block parallel imports on their own: Territorial clauses in dealer agreements have only contractual (obligatory) effect and cannot be used to assert trademark infringement against unrelated third parties such as parallel importers (IP High Court 2021). To block parallel imports, the only realistic measures are: (i) strengthening controls at the manufacturing stage (ensuring manufacturing outside the licensed territory is prohibited and enforceable), or (ii) creating a genuine quality or specification difference between genuine imported goods and domestic products.

(4) Price differentials and consumer interests: The root cause of parallel import issues is the price differential between domestic and foreign markets. Using trademark rights to maintain price differentials requires a genuine difference in origin or quality—which tends to undermine the attractiveness of parallel imports rather than enhance it. It is important to clearly communicate to overseas rights holders that trademark rights cannot function as a tool to protect pricing policies.

(5) Comparison with the EU and US: In the EU, the exhaustion doctrine applies—where genuine goods are placed on the EEA market, trademark rights are exhausted and intra-EEA parallel imports in principle do not constitute infringement. In the US, cases such as Levi Strauss v. Costco focus on material differences and quality as key issues. Japan's three-requirement doctrine shares conceptual common ground with EU and US approaches, and can be explained to overseas counsel as implementing the principle that parallel imports of genuine goods are lawful where they do not harm the source-indicating or quality-guarantee functions of the trademark.
practice.de:
Wichtige praktische Punkte zu Parallelimporten:

(1) Hauptanliegen ausländischer Rechtsinhaber: Parallelimporte sind für ausländische Marken ein zentrales Thema der Preiskontrolle und Markenverwaltung. Die genaue Erklärung des japanischen Drei-Voraussetzungen-Doktrin ist entscheidend.

(2) Praktische Checkliste:
Voraussetzung 1: Wer hat die Waren hergestellt? War die Anbringung der Marke rechtmäßig? Kern ist die Rechtmäßigkeit des Herstellungsakts.
Voraussetzung 2: Identität oder wirtschaftliche Gleichwertigkeit zwischen ausländischem und japanischem Inhaber bestätigen.
Voraussetzung 3: Qualitätskontrolle und wesentliche Qualitätsunterschiede prüfen.

(3) Gebietseinschränkungen blockieren Parallelimporte nicht allein: Nur tatsächliche Qualitäts- oder Herkunftsunterschiede oder die Kontrolle der Herstellungsphase können helfen.

(4) Preisdifferenzen und Verbraucherinteressen: Markenrechte können keine Preispolitik schützen. Dies klar an ausländische Inhaber kommunizieren.

(5) EU/USA-Vergleich: EU-Erschöpfungsprinzip für EEA-Binnenmarkt. US-Recht: Materialunterschied als Schlüsselfrage. Japanische Drei-Voraussetzungen-Doktrin konzeptionell ähnlich.
practice.it:
Punti pratici chiave sulle importazioni parallele:

(1) Principale preoccupazione dei titolari stranieri: Le importazioni parallele sono centrali per il controllo dei prezzi e la gestione del marchio. Spiegare accuratamente la dottrina giapponese dei tre requisiti è fondamentale.

(2) Checklist pratica:
Requisito 1: Chi ha fabbricato i beni? L'apposizione del marchio era legale? Il cuore è la legalità dell'atto di fabbricazione.
Requisito 2: Confermare l'identità o l'equivalenza economica tra titolari straniero e giapponese.
Requisito 3: Valutare il controllo della qualità e le eventuali differenze sostanziali di qualità.

(3) Le restrizioni territoriali nei contratti non bloccano le importazioni parallele da sole: Solo differenze reali di qualità o origine, o controllo della fase di fabbricazione, possono aiutare.

(4) Differenziali di prezzo e interessi dei consumatori: I diritti di marchio non possono proteggere le politiche di prezzo. Comunicare chiaramente questo ai titolari stranieri.

(5) Confronto UE/USA: Principio di esaurimento UE per il mercato interno. Diritto USA: differenza materiale come questione chiave. La dottrina giapponese dei tre requisiti è concettualmente simile.
